news_evidence.title: NVIDIA Unveils Vera, the CPU for Agents
news_evidence.llm_reason: NVIDIA’s new CPU is data‑center/hardware ecosystem news. It could influence cloud workloads and vendor partnerships longer term and may affect demand dynamics for cloud infrastructure providers (including Oracle Cloud), but the announcement is not Oracle-specific and is unlikely to drive immediate short-term price moves for ORCL.

news_evidence.title: Texas emerges as US' new economic powerhouse amid California exodus
news_evidence.llm_reason: Regional/relocation trend story about Texas. Oracle moved its HQ to Austin in 2020, so the piece is tangentially relevant to corporate footprint and talent/real-estate dynamics, but provides no company-specific news that would affect ORCL share price within 1–10 trading days.

macro_evidence.title: Companies Push Employees to Use AI — Just Not Too Much
macro_evidence.transmission_channel: demand shift for AI/cloud services; corporate cost controls affecting cloud compute usage and IT budgets
macro_evidence.reason: Companies are encouraging AI use (supporting ongoing demand for enterprise AI software, cloud and managed services) but also gatekeeping access to limit rising compute costs. First-order: signaling continued adoption of generative AI boosts near-term demand for cloud & enterprise AI platforms (positive for Technology vendors). Second-order: tighter internal controls and cost-shepherding can mute near-term incremental cloud consumption. Net near-term effect is modestly positive for Technology broadly and for cloud-capable vendors like ORCL, but magnitude is small and dependent on how widespread and strict the gatekeeping is.
